preamble: The diphthongization of the close-mid back rounded vowel /o/ to the diphthong /ɔɪ/ is documented in the historical development of Yiddish, where Middle High German long /oː/ shifted to Standard Yiddish /ɔɪ/ (spelled ⟨oy⟩). This change parallels the broader pattern of long mid vowels becoming diphthongs in Yiddish, diverging from the Standard German development where /oː/ was retained.

languageExamples.language: Middle High German to Standard Yiddish

examples.note: High; MHG /oː/ (long close-mid back rounded vowel) became Yiddish /ɔɪ/.
Already; another instance of the MHG /oː/ → Yiddish /ɔɪ/ diphthongization.

sources: Katz, D. (1987). Grammar of the Yiddish Language. Duckworth.
